cause_of_action: OTHER CIVIL PETITIONS (for relief from gevernment code section 945.4)

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER ELIZABETH MALIZIA'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 945.4 IS GRANTED. PETITIONER HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR RELIEF FROM THE REQUIREMENT FOR FILING A TIMELY ADMINISTRATIVE CLAIM DUE TO HER PHYSICAL INCAPACITATION AND COGNITIVE AND RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HAS MADE NO EFFORT TO SHOW THAT IT WOULD SUFFER PREJUDICE IF MS. MALIZIA IS GRANTED THE RELIEF SHE SEEKS BY THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-07-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Petitioner'S Motion For Relief From Government Code Section 945.4; Declaration Of Sophia M. Aslami In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sophia M. Aslami, JD, of Mary Alexander & Associates, P.C., Tel. 415.433.4440, for petitioners;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ELIZABETH MALIZIA'S Motion For Relief From Government Code Section 945.4 is GRANTED. Petitioner has shown good cause for relief from the requirement for filing a timely administrative claim due to her physical incapacitation and cognitive and respondent City and County of San Francisco has made no effort to show that it would suffer prejudice if Ms. Malizia is granted the relief she seeks by this motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
